matched.boundary: FRE 801(c) — "offered to prove the truth of the matter asserted"
matched.implication:
Score on this prong may plateau below 100%. SME hints aligned
with treatise doctrine have been observed to REGRESS accuracy
on datasets using the narrower interpretation (we observed 90.4%
→ 87.2% on LegalBench `hearsay` after adding treatise hints).

Refining hints to match the dataset's interpretation is
contamination of the SME-guidance methodology. Accept the
ceiling honestly and report it as a known doctrinal-boundary
effect.
matched.suggested_action:
Flag this source as having a doctrinally-contested prong. Set
`expected_ceiling` in the fit-assessment report; do NOT suggest
treatise-style hints aimed at this boundary unless the SME
explicitly asks for them.

synonyms: statement made outside the current trial
synonym_families.note:
Statements made by the declarant in any setting other than the
witness stand at the current trial or hearing. Includes
depositions, prior trials, conversations, recordings, letters,
etc. Worth recognising as equivalents when the case narrative
uses informal phrasing.

synonyms: the person who made the statement
synonym_families.note:
The person who made the out-of-court statement. Real fact
patterns rarely use the term "declarant" verbatim.

suggested_hints.template:
Recognise the following synonym families when reading case narratives — all members of a family refer to the same underlying concept for prong-detection purposes:
  - "out-of-court statement" ≡ "statement made outside the current trial", "prior statement", "earlier statement", "statement made elsewhere"
  - "declarant" ≡ "the speaker", "the person who said", "the person who made the statement"
suggested_hints.rationale: Real drafting practice uses synonyms for canonical terms. The runtime LLM extractor needs domain-specific equivalences or it will miss the prong on cases that use alternative terminology.
